crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: RoA of banks to slip 10-15 bps to 1.15-1.2% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The Indian banking industry is expected to see its return on assets (RoA) slip to 1.15-1.2% this fiscal, from ~1.3% last fiscal,  owing to two factors: first, reduced treasury income due to rising bond yields and, second, pre-emptive provisioning by banks ahead of the transition to the expected cred

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Toll collection growth seen moderating to 5-7% amid West Asia conflict
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Percolating economic impact of the West Asia conflict and a consequent deceleration in commercial traffic is likely to moderate growth in toll collection by 150-200 basis points (bps) on-year this fiscal.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Vacancy in commercial office space to drop 50 bps this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The vacancy level in India’s Grade A commercial office space1 is expected to gradually decrease by approximately 50 basis points (bps) to 15.5-16.0% by the end of the current fiscal, driven by a steady demand-supply gap resulting from a healthy net leasing growth and the scheduled completion of ongo

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Our West Asia stress test of 34 sectors Prolonged conflict to shave ~200 bps off corporate profitability
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The protracted conflict in West Asia has been goading domestic companies to realign supply chains, navigate pricing issues, manage higher fuel and freight costs, and contend with a depreciating rupee.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Capital goods players' double-digit growth momentum to continue
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Sustained government spending, steady capacity expansion in power, mining, oil and gas, metal and auto-linked sectors, and increasing opportunities from emerging segments such as data centres and electric vehicle (EV) infrastructure will ensure a revenue growth of 12-14% for the capital goods indust

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Input costs on fire
crisil-research.description: The West Asia conflict has sparked the largest oil shock the world has seen so far. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has only broadened the shock to other input categories even as manufacturers are already grappling with higher costs from critical inputs such as copper and aluminium.

crisil-research.title: Time to overhaul branch expansion models
crisil-research.description: The process typically begins with gathering credit data to identify geographies with credit-active households, commercial density and visible signs of economic activity.

crisil-research.title: The CCAR drive
crisil-research.description: Regional banks in the United States nearing $100 billion in total consolidated assets should already be preparing for the Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), rather than waiting for the regulatory clock to start ticking.

crisil-research.title: The increasing complexity of underwriting in private banking
crisil-research.description: US regional banks are steadily expanding their private banking capabilities, making deliberate, expensive bets on the segment as the next engine of profitable growth. And at the heart of that bet lies a critical capability: to underwrite complex, bespoke credit for high and ultra-high net worth (HNW

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Crude jolt
crisil-research.description: A 34.7% on-year surge in petroleum exports helped India’s goods exports rise 13.8% on-year in April to $43.6 billion (vs a 7.4% contraction in March). Core1 exports were resilient, rising 10.4% on-year to $31.6 billion, albeit on a low base.

crisil-research.title: Quickonomics: Oil's not well
crisil-research.description: India’s crude oil trade deficit has been under the pump historically because of having to meet over 85% of its annual requirement from imports.

baroda-etrade-sor.title: First Light | Hindustan Unilever, Adani Ports, Greenply Industries, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Jindal Steel, Laurus Labs, Mphasis, Kajaria Ceramics, Equitas Small Finance Bank, Smartworks Coworking Spaces

KMB: Steady advances growth with improving asset quality. JSP: Results better than expectations on strong volume growth. LAURUS: A long runway for C

ssga-insights.title: The courage to follow the fundamentals
ssga-insights.description: Look past the noise—focus on what drives markets. Growth and earnings remain strong, even as the backdrop supporting them shifts.

ssga-insights.title: Position beyond US large-cap tech for the next wave of AI and economic realignment
ssga-insights.description: Counter market concentration by broadening your equity exposure to include US small caps, emerging markets, defense companies, and businesses at the forefront of the AI CapEx infrastructure buildout.

ssga-insights.title: Diversify for a new regime with multi-asset strategies and real assets
ssga-insights.description: As stock-bond correlations rise, diversification must evolve. Discover how multi-asset strategies and real assets can improve portfolio resilience.

ssga-insights.title: Prioritize income and resilience with active short duration and multi-sector credit
ssga-insights.description: Amid rate volatility and credit spreads remaining tight, focusing on selectivity and active positioning can help generate returns through income and carry.

ssga-insights.title: 2026 Midyear ETF Market Outlook: Resilient fundamentals, rising fragility
ssga-insights.description: Earnings—the lifeblood of this bull market—continue to drive the multiyear rally in risk assets, even as the macro backdrop grows more challenging.

ssga-insights.title: The new era of income investing
ssga-insights.description: Rethink income investing—how diversified portfolio strategies beyond bonds can generate resilient cash flow and sustainable income across market cycles.

ssga-insights.title: Consistency as a compass in developed market equities
ssga-insights.description: Using Information Ratio to select disciplined, risk-adjusted strategies in developed market equity portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: Be ready for any market with liquid ETFs
ssga-insights.description: In volatile markets, liquidity is vital. And with more uncertainty likely ahead, you can get in and out quickly, easily, and at an attractive cost with State Street ETFs.

ssga-insights.title: Eurozone signals caution on June rate hike
ssga-insights.description: PMI weakness and softer inflation reshape rate paths across Europe and Japan, as demand destruction and policy shifts drive caution among major central banks.

ssga-insights.title: US–China trade truce holds, but for how long?
ssga-insights.description: Trump’s China visit signals trade war pause holds, but normalization unlikely as China’s dominance and surplus risk conflicts.

ssga-insights.title: Global markets tilt toward AI
ssga-insights.description: AI-driven gains reshape markets, but rising concentration risks challenge diversification across equities and traditional assets.

ssga-insights.title: Risk-on is back, higher volatility stocks rebound
ssga-insights.description: Equities have recovered their poise, with high-volatility stocks benefiting from a renewed risk-on rally, despite geopolitical and economic uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: What’s driving the surge in global interest rates?
ssga-insights.description: We discuss how oil prices, US inflation, and geopolitical tensions are impacting rates, and look at rates under 3 key geopolitical scenarios.

ssga-insights.title: Equities back to overweight
ssga-insights.description: We modestly increase equity exposure, moving back to overweight, while raising both total portfolio risk and active risk positioning.

ssga-insights.title: Japan is back, and how!
ssga-insights.description: Japan’s revival is gaining traction as political stability, firmer inflation, and semiconductor-led investment support a more durable growth regime.

ssga-insights.title: Equity duration matters, but earnings decide
ssga-insights.description: US equities outperform as resilient earnings offset headwinds, while valuation and FX pressures weigh on ex‑US markets.

ssga-insights.title: US inflation surprises but signals unclear
ssga-insights.description: Hot inflation, resilient UK growth, and Japan’s tightening path shape the outlook as markets weigh data noise against rising rates and geopolitical risks.

ssga-insights.title: Systematic High Quality Corporate Fixed Income: Q1 2026 commentary
ssga-insights.description: In a period marked by volatility, SAFI’s disciplined, systematic signals (value and momentum) helped deliver resilient performance and consistent alpha.

ssga-insights.title: Why Canada matters for developed ex-US equity allocation
ssga-insights.description: Investors using MSCI EAFE Index for their international allocation are missing out on exposure to Canada, the world’s ninth largest economy. Here’s why MSCI World ex USA may be a better option.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: April 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt rebounded in April as improving risk sentiment lifted both hard and local currency debt, though geopolitical tensions and oil kept markets cautious.

ssga-insights.title: How to determine your risk tolerance (and why it matters)
ssga-insights.description: What is risk tolerance in investing? Learn how to determine your risk tolerance, avoid panic selling, and build an investment approach you can stick to over the long term.

ssga-insights.title: Actively managed ETFs: A new chapter for global investors
ssga-insights.description: Actively managed ETFs are gaining rapid global adoption as investors seek active insight with the efficiency and flexibility of the ETF wrapper. Get the details.

ssga-insights.title: US labor data improve but risks remain ahead
ssga-insights.description: US labor data surprised to the upside last week, but mixed signals remain. Canada’s labor market stayed weak, while Australia signaled a pause before further tightening.

ssga-insights.title: Market pricing of UK growth is too optimistic
ssga-insights.description: Slowing migration, an ageing workforce and weak productivity threaten UK growth, reshaping fiscal dynamics and altering the outlook for gilts, equities and sterling.

ssga-insights.title: Tokenized money market funds (MMFs): Revolutionizing liquidity management
ssga-insights.description: Tokenized money market funds are changing how institutions move cash—helping to improve liquidity mobility, reducing friction, and making collateral more usable.

ssga-insights.title: Markets shift to selectivity phase
ssga-insights.description: Strong earnings persist, but markets are turning more selective, rewarding durable cash flows and penalizing crowded leadership.

ssga-insights.title: Tactical shift favors USD
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitical risk dominated April FX, with Iran tensions and oil volatility. We turned tactically positive on the USD amid resilient US growth and uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: What is sector investing?
ssga-insights.description: Discover sector investing—and how it can help investors express their market views and target trends to capture upside potential without putting too much into any one stock.

ssga-insights.title: Monthly Cash Review – USD
ssga-insights.description: April calmed after March, but oil swings kept volatility alive. The Fed stayed on hold and signaled higher-for-longer; we modestly extended to lock in yield.

ssga-insights.title: Risk-on ETF flows in a river of hills market
ssga-insights.description: Markets surged in April amid volatility, with global equities and ETFs seeing strong flows as risk assets climbed despite uncertain headlines.

icici-bank-research.description: US CPI inflation rose above expectations in April printing at 3.8% YoY (exp: 3.7%) driven by food and energy prices. On a sequential basis, prices rose by 0.6% MoM. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.description: India has raised import tariffs on gold and silver to 15%, reversing the 2024 duty cuts, as the government moves to curb surging precious metal imports. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.description: The ceasefire between the US and Iran reached a particularly precarious moment Monday as President Donald Trump said the agreement was on “massive life support” after he rejected Tehran’s latest peace offer. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.description: President Donald Trump said he would pause a US-led effort to help stranded ships exit the Strait of Hormuz to see if an agreement with Iran to end the war could be reached. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.description: The US and Iran exchanged fire in a flareup of violence on Monday that also drew in the United Arab Emirates, prompting calls for renewed strikes on Iranian targets and casting doubt on the fate of a four-week ceasefire. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: Fixed income weekly: Global developments in focus
icici-bank-research.description: Domestic yields continued to move upwards last week in response to higher global yields as the lack of clarity on the ceasefire in the Middle East drive oil prices higher.  Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.title: US GDP rebounded in Q1 driven by a surge in AI investments
icici-bank-research.description: The US economy rebounded in Q1 printing at 2.0% QoQsaar (expectations: 2.2%) from 0.5% in Q42025 driven by a surge in AI related investments and a normalization in government spending. Net exports worked as a drag as imports surged outpacing export growth. Read the report to know more.

icici-bank-research.description: Oil prices eased on Monday after US President Donald Trump said the United States would begin an effort to free up ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, however the lack of a US-Iran peace deal kept prices supported above USD 100/bbl.  Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Energy proved the main driver of the continued rise in Italian headline inflation in May. Core inflation also edged up marginally. We expect some contained pass-through over the next few months, with average yearly inflation around the 3% mark

ing-think-rss.description: FRANCE: France’s economic outlook is deteriorating rapidly. With inflation edging higher again and domestic demand weakening, a recession now looks increasingly likely

ing-think-rss.description: JAPAN: Today's data in Japan was quite surprising. Despite soaring global energy prices, the economy appears to have held up well. goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Energy Crunch Could Accelerate Europe’s Shift to Electrification
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Energy] A European push to reduce its reliance on imported natural gas and oil could boost electrification and prove an earnings boon to utilities investing in cleaner sources.

goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Rising prices driven by disruptions to the flow of oil from the Middle East are having a measurable impact on US consumer spending.

goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Goldman Sachs Research forecasts the total addressable market (TAM) for the AI networking sector to increase by 9 times to $154 billion.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Will the Corporate Investment in AI Pay Off?
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] While consumer AI adoption has been spectacular, most users rely on free versions. Successful enterprise adoption will be critical for AI economics to work, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: China’s Exports Are Expected to Slow Before Green Energy Surge
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] China's export growth is under near-term pressure as the Middle East energy shock dampens demand. But the crisis may accelerate global clean energy adoption, a business China dominates.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why UK Gilt Yields Are Climbing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] UK government bond yields have soared amid growing political uncertainty, with questions over the future direction of fiscal policy.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: US Data Center Power Demand Projected to Double by 2027
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] US data center power demand is forecast to surge from 31 GW in 2025 to 66 GW by 2027, driven by AI expansion, with implications for regional electricity markets and grid reliability.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Securing and Financing the Future of Water
AI Agents Forecast to Boost Tech Cash Flow as Usage Soars

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Korea’s Stock Market Is Forecast to Set Fresh Highs
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Goldman Sachs Research lifted its KOSPI target to 9,000, citing a 300% earnings surge and a supercycle in memory chips that could power Asia’s rally.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Stock Markets Are Increasingly Vulnerable to Rising Bond Yields
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] A surge in global bond yields is increasing the risk of a stock market correction, particularly if the economic backdrop deteriorates.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: How Hedge Funds Are Trading Semiconductor Stocks
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Some hedge funds are taking profits on makers of semiconductors and related equipment to manage risk in their portfolios, even as tech stocks climb.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The S&P 500 Is Forecast to Climb as Earnings Growth Powers Stocks Higher
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Goldman Sachs Research raised its S&P 500 year-end target to 8000, projecting earnings per share of $340 in 2026 (a 24% increase, year over year). AI infrastructure investment is expected to account for about half of the earnings growth.

jpmorgan-research.title: US–Israel military operation against Iran: Are markets on edge?
jpmorgan-research.description: The conflict could pose an upside risk for oil, gold and aluminum prices — though uncertainties around its longer-term impact remain.
